intro: Ancylanthos lactiflorus, commonly known as the white Ancylanthos, is a flowering plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, contributing to its potential health benefits, such as wound healing, skin protection, and the alleviation of fever and pain. The bioactive constituents of Ancylanthos lactiflorus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Ancylanthos lactiflorus is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.
